beats.activity: Wake up, shower, make coffee, review lesson plans for the week.
beats.reasoning: Early riser habit; full energy on Monday morning. Preps mentally for the teaching week ahead.

beats.activity: Walk to school, enjoy the sunny morning and scan rooftops for birds.
beats.location_name: Lincoln High School (South Lake Union, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Same neighborhood commute on foot; sunny weather makes birdwatching walk appealing and energizes the day.

beats.activity: Teach first three periods of the day (biology and chemistry classes).
beats.location_name: Lincoln High School (South Lake Union, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Standard Monday morning teaching block; wraps up just before the 11am FIFA match, which Layla does not plan to attend (focuses on work commitments over sports viewing).

beats.activity: Grab lunch from a nearby cafe and eat at school while prepping afternoon labs.
beats.location_name: Molly Moon's Ice Cream (South Lake Union, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Quick lunch errand within the school neighborhood; avoids downtown bar crowds watching the FIFA match, staying focused on work preparation.

beats.activity: Teach afternoon periods and oversee lab work in chemistry class.
beats.location_name: Lincoln High School (South Lake Union, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Afternoon teaching block; students conducting hands-on experiments keeps the day engaging and structured.

beats.activity: Walk home via Capitol Hill neighborhood, stopping at a local park to photograph birds with film camera.
beats.reasoning: Slight detour on the walk home; sunny weather and high energy make this a perfect window for birdwatching and photography, a core passion.
Return to SLU; walk allows mental wind-down from teaching and time to process the day.

beats.activity: Work on home brewing project: prep ingredients and sanitize equipment for an IPA batch.
beats.reasoning: Relaxing hands-on hobby at home; perfect Monday afternoon activity that allows Layla to decompress while pursuing a core interest.

beats.activity: Prepare and eat dinner; text close friend about plans for the week.
beats.reasoning: Quiet evening meal at home; values close friendships and uses this time to check in with trusted friends rather than seeking big social scenes.

beats.activity: Review lesson materials for tomorrow, journal about the day, relax with a book and herbal tea.
beats.reasoning: Wind-down evening routine at home; anxious-but-high-achieving personality drives lesson prep, balanced with journaling and reading for self-care.

diary: Monday morning felt like a gift—that perfect Seattle sunshine hitting the rooftops as I walked to Lincoln High. Spotted a pileated woodpecker near the school, which got me excited enough to mention it to my bio class (they were less thrilled than I was). The day hummed along: labs, questions, the usual Monday chaos of students still half-asleep. I grabbed lunch at Molly Moon's and prepped experiments while eating, which felt efficient but also a little lonely. By afternoon, the chemistry class was actually engaged with their work, which made the day feel worth it. After school, I detoured through Volunteer Park with my film camera—the light was perfect, and I managed to catch a few decent shots of crows and a heron. Home felt welcoming after that. Spent the evening prepping for my IPA batch, something meditative about sanitizing equipment and measuring hops. Texted Maya about weekend plans. Ended the night with my journal and a book. Tired but content.
thought_process: I chose to linger in Volunteer Park because the weather was too good to waste, and honestly, I needed that quiet time with the camera after a full day of managing classroom energy. The World Cup match didn't really pull at me—I'm not much of a soccer person, and the bars downtown felt like a different world from my routine. The Light Rail closure didn't affect me since I walk everywhere in South Lake, but I was aware of it, wondered if it frustrated my colleagues. My mood shifted from routine-focused this morning to genuinely present by the afternoon; the bird photography and home brewing project felt like they anchored me back into what I actually care about. Teaching's rewarding but draining, so those evening hours alone with my hands busy and my mind winding down felt essential.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
